title: config.v1
description: The per-service deployment-config declaration: every environment key a service consumes, classed as required-explicit (boot fails loud if unset), defaulted (the code default, documented), or capability (a named optional capability whose tri-state — configured / not_configured / misconfigured — is computed from the env, optionally live-probed, exposed on /health, and consulted by capability-gated endpoints). The declaration is the SSOT the deploy surfaces (compose / helm / lite) and the boot preflight are checked against (gate:config-contract).

service.description: The adopting service's name (compose service / helm component / lite program).

keys.description: Every environment key this service reads (or brokers), one declaration each.

capabilities.description: The named optional capabilities the `capability`-classed keys group into.

surface_only.description: Keys a deploy surface sets on this service that the service does NOT read — documented drift (each entry names why it is still there), so the surface↔declaration check stays honest without silently ignoring anything.

Target.description: A deploy surface: deploy/compose (docker-compose.yml + .env.example), deploy/helm (chart templates/values), deploy/lite (entrypoint.sh + supervisord.conf).

class.description: required-explicit: unset/empty ⇒ the boot preflight raises and the deploy refuses to start. defaulted: the documented code default applies when unset. capability: the key belongs to the named capability; the service runs either way and the capability's tri-state is computed from the env.

default.description: The effective default when unset (required for class=defaulted; may describe a derivation, e.g. "(derived from AGENT_IMAGE)").

capability.description: The capability this key belongs to (required for class=capability; must name an entry in `capabilities`).

secret.description: The value is a credential (P14): never logged, never in goldens; deploy surfaces feed it from a secret store.

targets.description: Which deploy surfaces plumb this key (default: all three). An EMPTY array = an in-code dial deliberately not exposed by any deploy surface (still declared, so the undeclared-read scan stays tight).

mode.description: all (default): configured ⇔ every member key set; SOME-but-not-all set ⇒ misconfigured. any: configured ⇔ at least one member key set (alternative credential paths); env-only state is never misconfigured (a probe can still demote to misconfigured).

when_unconfigured.description: The observable degraded behavior: what capability-gated endpoints answer (the typed, actionable error) when the capability is not configured.

Probe.description: A cheap LIVE verification that set values actually WORK — so set-but-failing credentials show as `misconfigured` on /health BEFORE any workload runs (the silent-401 STT incident). Probes run only when the env-level state is already `configured`; they execute at boot (logged, never boot-blocking) and lazily on /health when the cached result is older than `ttl_s`. A probe failure demotes the /health row to `misconfigured` with a reason; it never changes the service's own `status`.

timeout_s.description: Per-attempt budget (default 2s — must stay under the deploy healthcheck timeout).

ttl_s.description: Cache lifetime (default 60s): /health refreshes the probe at most this often.

http.description: One authenticated request to the capability's endpoint. Verdict, in order: a network failure/timeout ⇒ probe FAILS (unreachable); an `unauthorized_statuses` answer ⇒ FAILS (the credential was REJECTED); an `exhausted_statuses` answer ⇒ FAILS (the credential is authentic but cannot buy the work); an `invalid_statuses` answer ⇒ FAILS (we are not talking to the declared endpoint at all — the URL shape is wrong); ANY other status (400/405/…) proves the endpoint is reachable and the credential accepted ⇒ probe passes.

url_key.description: the declared key holding the endpoint URL — accepted as a bare base (https://host) OR as the full endpoint already carrying `path`; the probe appends `path` only when absent, the same rule the bot's client and the terminal dictation route apply

path.description: the capability's endpoint path (e.g. /v1/audio/transcriptions), appended to the configured URL only when it does not already end with it

auth_key.description: the declared key holding the bearer token sent as Authorization

unauthorized_statuses.description: statuses that mean the credential was REJECTED (default [401, 403])

invalid_statuses.description: statuses that mean the URL does not name this capability's endpoint — proof of the WRONG address, not of a working one (default []; the stt transcriptions probe declares [404], since a real OpenAI-compatible transcriptions path answers 400/401 to an empty body and never 404)

exhausted_statuses.description: statuses that mean the credential AUTHENTICATED but cannot pay for the work — a metered backend's 402 (default []; the stt probe declares [402]). Distinct from unauthorized: nothing is wrong with the token, it simply buys nothing, so every request fails while every reachability check passes. Only meaningful with `payload: "audio"` — an endpoint cannot price a body it never received.

payload.description: send a REAL request body instead of an empty POST. `audio` posts a ~1s WAV as multipart/form-data — the same shape a bot's first chunk makes — because an empty body cannot distinguish a funded credential from a worthless one: a metered backend answers both alike. It also makes the verdict independent of WHOSE token it is (a billing-exempt account and a funded one both answer 200), so no account identity is encoded anywhere. The request is METERED — declare a long `ttl_s`.

payload_model.description: the `model` form field sent with `payload: "audio"` (default whisper-1); backends that validate model ids need their served name

file.description: Verifies a credentials FILE as visible to THIS service. Skipped (not failed) when `path_key` is unset — e.g. a mode:any capability satisfied by an API key instead. Verdict: a regular, readable, non-empty JSON file ⇒ passes; a DIRECTORY (docker's bind-mount of a missing host path), unreadable, or non-JSON content ⇒ FAILS (misconfigured). `fallback_paths` lets a containerized service check a mirror mount of a docker-HOST path it cannot see directly.

path_key.description: the declared key holding the path (HOST-path semantics documented on the key)

fallback_paths.description: in-container mirror locations checked when the key's own path is not visible locally
